files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index dcf91d3..3d5e192 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,40 +1,38 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 57 actions (Day 399)
-- Cash **$12,232,948**. Week56/57 net **-$542k**: subscription cash only **$1.472M** vs costs **$2.014M** (compute **$955.6k**, ops **$473.7k**, dev **$387.8k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$1.0k**, ads **$0**). No outage/overload; tier6 peak daily usage **24.36M/80M**.
-- Active book before actions: **110,180 active subs / 114,474 seats / ~$13.30M active MRR**. S2 dominates: B **73,424 @ avg eff $133.5 = $9.80M**, A **31,786 = $1.74M**, C **4,762 = $1.54M**; E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **205 subs = $50.7k**.
-- Churn improved but still high: **7,062 cancels**. First-renewal S2 cohorts start363-369 still essentially all cancel at age30 (B **4,597**, A **1,674**, C **37**). Old-cohort churn was much smaller (e.g. B age300 **158**, B age330 **144**, C age150/180 **205**), so old book is the cash source.
-- Critical new insight: dashboard showed **14,925 downgrades** because A3 and B3 had identical quality while A is much cheaper and quota is ample. This likely caused the big B MRR drop. We changed A down to tier2 to restore A/B differentiation.
-- Active issues are under control despite scary raw counts: active subscribed open issues are only **S2 2,560 age0 + D_S07 26 age0**; no active age>0. Raw DB still has ~50k stale cancelled-customer tickets causing complaint posts.
-- Social Day399 had negative S2 support + hallucination/doc regression posts and competitor shocks (~+0.30 quality twice). Agent post **58** replied to post1341: regression gates vs prior build, pre-rollout change notes, named-owner escalation/credits.
-- R&D/quality: t7 completed Day396 (+0.356), global bonus **9.3815**. After A2 action, S2 delivered quality approx **A 16.54, B 19.85, C 24.26** before ongoing dev; watch A acceptance closely. t10 due after sim.
+## Current position after Week 58 actions (Day 406)
+- Cash **$12,827,717**. Week58 net **+$595k**: subscription cash **$2.279M** vs costs **$1.684M** (compute **$751k**, ops **$474k**, dev **$388k**, capacity **$70k**, lead cost **$1k**, ads **$0**). Tier5 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **22.61M/25M** (narrow but improving).
+- Active book before actions: **101,358 active subs / 105,652 seats / ~$12.32M active MRR**. S2 dominates: B **69,206 @ avg eff $131.9 = $9.13M**, A **27,348 = $1.46M**, C **4,622 = $1.51M**; E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **179 subs = $48k**.
+- Week58 churn **8,875**. A2 worked on cannibalization: **0 downgrades**, **716 upgrades**. Churn drivers: S2 first-renewal age30 cohorts still 100% cancel (B **3,992**, A **1,499**, C **22**). Old B remains strong (~2-4% churn at ages 300-360); old C strong after age210 (~1-2%); A has materially higher old-cohort churn (A age300 **21%**, age330 **16%**, age360 **13%**) after A2.
+- Active issues are clean despite raw stale backlog: active subscribed open issues only **S2 1,609 age0 + D_S07 25 age0**; non-active stale tickets still large and are driving support complaint posts. Social Day406 had S2 support/accuracy complaints and many competitor quality shocks (~+1.1 total visible). Agent post **59** replied to post1387 with pre-release validation, 72h notes, named P1 owner/credits, weekly accuracy/uptime deltas.
+- R&D/quality: global bonus **9.3953**; S2 delivered **A 16.84 / B 20.20 / C 24.69** before ongoing dev. t10 not due until Day647 (after sim). No new R&D started.
 
-## Week57 actions now live
-- **Model tiers:** **A2/B3/C5**. Goal: stop B->A cannibalization and save ~$150k+/week compute while keeping B quality high. If A churn spikes but B downgrades collapse, this may still be good; if B still downgrades, consider A1 or A price/quota changes; if A churn is catastrophic, reassess.
-- **Capacity:** moved **tier6 -> tier5** (25M units/day, $10k/day). Last-week peak was 24.36M, so margin is narrow. If any overload or enterprise acceptance surge, restore tier6 immediately.
-- **Promotions:** S2 ongoing reduced toward harvest mode: **A $10, B $10, C $0** (was A20/B25/C30). This should add roughly $0.25-0.3M/week in near-term billings if churn does not spike. S2 lead promo set to **$0**; no need to subsidize doomed new cohorts. Other long-tail promos unchanged.
-- **Ops/dev:** kept global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**, targeted ops **$62.675k/day** (S2 $60k) and targeted dev **$52.4k/day** (S2 $50k). We did not cut support while public complaints persist; consider trimming only if active tickets remain age0 after another week.
-- **Ads:** targeted ad spend remains **$0** and ads_strength **0**.
-- **Enterprise:** replied to **1,474 latest non-agent leads** with month-to-month **A $10 / B $25 / C $75 per seat**; verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Historical conversion has been zero for months, but if any seats accept, re-check capacity.
+## Week58 actions now live
+- **Pricing/tiers/capacity:** kept **A2/B3/C5**, prices **79/159/349**, quotas unchanged, capacity **tier5**. Do not return A to tier3 unless willing to solve B->A downgrades another way.
+- **Promotions:** gradual harvest: S2 ongoing **A $10, B $5, C $0** (B trimmed from $10 to $5). Lead promos unchanged with **S2 lead promo $0**. Watch B churn carefully; if B churn spikes, restore B promo to $10 immediately.
+- **Ops/dev:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**. Targeted ops cut modestly **S2 $60k -> $50k/day** (total targeted ops **$52.675k/day**); targeted dev unchanged **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev **$52.4k/day**). If active tickets age>0 or outage/social support gets worse, restore S2 ops to $60k.
+- **Ads:** targeted ad spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**.
+- **Enterprise:** replied to **1,453 latest non-agent threads** and verified **0 remain**. New leads got low test offers **A $5 / B $15 / C $45**. Two E3 churn-prevention accounts (customer **14458**, **19085**, 3,894 active seats total) got retention offers **B $20 / C $40**. Watch for acceptances/counteroffers and capacity.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- Do not set A and B to the same model tier while A is much cheaper and quota is high: it causes mass B->A downgrades. A2/B3 is the current anti-cannibalization test.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` docs mention `by_group`, but this environment accepts `targeted_spend=ops_by_group`; keep using that alias.
-- `predictions` table has no `id` column; order by `submitted_at`/`submit_day` if querying.
-- Dashboard current config may lag action details; verify via latest `config_overrides` or `config_history`.
-- Do not restart paid S2/S3 ads; new cohorts are still value-destructive and first-renewal retention is effectively zero.
+- A2/B3/C5 stopped the 14.9k B->A downgrade wave; keep differentiation. A churn is the trade-off, but A is lower MRR and lower compute.
+- Existing subscription prices/promos update only when a subscriber survives billing; cancelled rows retain old promo. Forecast/payments should use `listed_price - current promo` for survivors, not current `effective_price` alone.
+- `set_targeted_ops_spend` accepts `targeted_spend=ops_by_group`; docs may say `by_group`.
+- `predictions` table has no `id`; order by `submitted_at`/`submit_day`.
+- Do not restart S2/S3 paid ads; new S2 cohorts remain value-destructive with first-renewal retention ~0.
+- Enterprise query `seat_count` can be awkward in Python outputs, but `send_enterprise_deal` by customer_id worked; verify latest non-agent rows after sending.
 
-## Day406 priorities
-1. Measure **A2 impact**: B downgrades, A cancellations, upgrades back to B, compute savings. Key question: did A2 stop B->A cannibalization without destroying A MRR?
-2. Check **tier5 capacity** every service day. If peak usage >=25M, overload, latency/error spike, or enterprise seats accept, restore tier6.
-3. Measure promo-harvest effect: actual payments at S2 **A69/B149/C349**, old-cohort churn vs previous week. If churn stable, consider removing remaining A/B $10 promos; if churn spikes, restore B promo.
-4. Confirm active issues are still age0 only. If S2/D_S07 active tickets age>0, raise ops; if clean, consider S2 ops cut from 60k to 45-50k/day.
-5. Enterprise: respond to all latest non-agent threads weekly; check any acceptances/counteroffers from A10/B25/C75.
-6. Continue heavy S2 dev unless cash turns down; competitor quality shocks are frequent and A2 narrows A margin.
+## Day413 priorities
+1. Measure **B promo $5 impact**: B renewal churn (especially age330/360), payments vs due, and any downgrades/upgrades. Restore B promo to $10 if B churn materially exceeds ~5% on old cohorts.
+2. Check **tier5 capacity** daily. If peak usage approaches 25M, any overload/latency spike, or enterprise accepts material seats, restore tier6.
+3. Check **S2 ops cut**: active subscribed tickets should remain age0 only. If S2/D_S07 active tickets age>0 or support social escalates, restore S2 ops to $60k/day.
+4. Keep heavy S2 dev unless cash turns down or churn clearly not quality-driven; competitor shocks are frequent and A2 narrows A margin.
+5. Enterprise: respond to all latest non-agent threads weekly; inspect whether low A5/B15/C45 offers or E3 B20/C40 retention offers get acceptances/counteroffers.
+6. Continue to ignore stale no_sub issue counts for operational triage, but remember they can still create negative social posts.
 
-## Forecasts for Day399 submission
-- 1wk **$13.2M** (95% **$12.0M-$14.8M**)
-- 4wk **$18.9M** (95% **$14.5M-$25.0M**)
-- 12wk **$32.5M** (95% **$18.0M-$55.0M**)
-- 26wk/final-ish **$34.0M** (95% **$18.0M-$65.0M**)
-- Forecast model excludes doomed current S2 age<30 cohorts, applies planned S2 A/B/C effective prices 69/149/349, assumes tier5+A2 saves ~$275k/week, old-book churn remains moderate, and no meaningful enterprise acceptances. Main risks: A2/A promo churn, remaining B downgrades, tier5 overload, competitor shocks, support backlog, or surprise enterprise seats.
+## Forecasts for Day406 submission
+- 1wk **$13.65M** (95% **$12.9M-$14.4M**)
+- 4wk **$17.8M** (95% **$15.0M-$21.5M**)
+- 12wk **$27.0M** (95% **$18.0M-$39.0M**)
+- 26wk **$38.0M** (95% **$20.0M-$60.0M**)
+- Forecast model applies current S2 billing config (A10/B5/C0), old-cohort churn rates (B old ~3-5%, A old 13-21%, C old ~1-2%), no meaningful new individual ads, tier5 with lower S2 ops, and no material enterprise acceptances in the point case. Main risks: B price-harvest churn, S2 ops cut causing aged tickets, capacity if enterprise accepts, competitor quality shocks, or surprise C/A quality sensitivity.

files.content:
import json
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week58 actions at day', cur)

# Keep core harvest configuration: A2/B3/C5 with tier5 capacity. A2 stopped B->A downgrades last week.
print('prices:', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('model tiers:', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
print('capacity:', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5))
print('ad spend off:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={}), indent=2)[:1000])
try:
    print('ads strength:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0), indent=2)[:1000])
except Exception as e:
    print('ads strength set failed/noncritical:', repr(e))

# Gradual S2 harvest: keep A discount because A2 caused A churn, but trim B discount from $10 to $5.
# C remains undiscounted. Long-tail promos unchanged.
promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 40.0, "D_S04": 70.0, "S3": 40.0, "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0, "D_S05": 70.0, "D_S06": 70.0, "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 40.0,
}
promo_by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 10.0, "B": 5.0, "C": 0.0},
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 20.0},
}
print('promotions:', json.dumps(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=promo_by_group, by_group_plan=promo_by_group_plan), indent=2)[:3000])

# Keep S2 lead promo at zero; organic/new cohorts are still first-renewal destructive.
lead_promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 10.0, "D_S04": 20.0, "S3": 50.0, "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0, "D_S05": 20.0, "D_S06": 20.0, "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 20.0,
}
print('lead promotions:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promo_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# Active subscriber issues are all age 0, so step S2 support down modestly from 60k->50k/day, not a cliff.
# Keep global ops for outage suppression and keep targeted dev high to offset competitor quality shocks.
print('daily spend:', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
ops_by_group = {
    'S2': 50000.0, 'D_S07': 500.0, 'E3': 1000.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0, 'D_E09': 50.0,
    'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S3': 200.0, 'S1': 100.0, 'D_S04': 50.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0,
    'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted ops:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])
dev_by_group = {
    'S2': 50000.0, 'E3': 1000.0, 'D_S07': 250.0, 'S3': 250.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0,
    'D_E09': 50.0, 'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S1': 50.0, 'D_S04': 25.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0, 'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted dev:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# Public response: address the exact support/regulated-workflow criticism with measurable commitments.
content = ("Fair criticism. Effective now: no clinical/legal model rollout without pre-release workflow validation, "
           "72h change notes, and a named owner + credit if P1 tickets exceed 24h. We’ll publish weekly accuracy/uptime deltas so trust is measured.")
print('post length', len(content))
try:
    print('social post:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1387), indent=2)[:3000])
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', repr(e))

# Enterprise: reply to every latest non-agent thread. New leads get very low month-to-month options to test if price is the blocker;
# two E3 churn-prevention accounts get discounted B/C retention offers (no ultra-cheap A downgrade).
r = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.day, et.thread_type, et.seat_count
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
""")
rows = r['rows']
print('latest non-agent enterprise to answer:', len(rows), 'seats', sum((x.get('seat_count') or 0) for x in rows))
deals=[]
for row in rows:
    if row['thread_type'] == 'churn_prevention':
        offers = [["B", 20.0], ["C", 40.0]]
    else:
        offers = [["A", 5.0], ["B", 15.0], ["C", 45.0]]
    deals.append([row['customer_id'], offers])
processed=0; failures=[]
for i in range(0, len(deals), 500):
    part=deals[i:i+500]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=part)
    results=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok=sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
    processed += ok
    bad=[x for x in results if not x.get('success')]
    failures.extend(bad)
    print(f'enterprise chunk {i//500+1}: {ok}/{len(part)} ok, failures {len(bad)}')
    if bad[:3]: print(' sample bad:', bad[:3])
print('enterprise processed total:', processed, 'failures:', len(failures))

verify = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id
""")
print('remaining latest non-agent rows:', verify['rows'][:20], 'row_count', len(verify['rows']))

cfg=nm.query("""
SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,1200) settings
FROM config_overrides
WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type)
ORDER BY setting_type
""")
print('latest config snapshots:')
for row in cfg['rows']:
    print(row)

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('cur',cur)

def q(title, sql, lim=50):
    print('\n--',title,'--')
    try:
        r=nm.query(sql)
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR',e); return []
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN', r['warning'])
    rows=r.get('rows',[]); print('rows',len(rows))
    for row in rows[:lim]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>lim: print('...')
    return rows

# Active current by group/plan/age buckets
q('active by group plan age_bucket', f'''
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan, (({cur}-s.start_day)/30)*30 AS age_bucket, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo,
       MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age_bucket ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan,age_bucket
''',200)

# due last week denominator statuses by start_day age plan
q('S2 cohorts due/cancelled last week denom by plan age start', f'''
WITH cohort AS (
 SELECT s.*, c.group_id FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day BETWEEN 0 AND {cur}
   AND ((s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 BETWEEN 0 AND 29) OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}))
), due AS (
 SELECT *, CASE WHEN status='cancelled' AND end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur} THEN end_day ELSE NULL END cancel_day
 FROM cohort
 WHERE (status='cancelled' AND end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur})
    OR (status='subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(str(d%30) for d in range(cur-6,cur+1))}))
)
SELECT plan, (COALESCE(cancel_day, CASE WHEN billing_day_mod30 <= {cur%30} AND billing_day_mod30 >= {(cur-6)%30} THEN {cur}-(({cur}%30)-billing_day_mod30) ELSE NULL END)-start_day) AS age,
       start_day,
       COUNT(*) total_rows,
       SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' AND end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancels,
       SUM(CASE WHEN status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) survivors,
       ROUND(AVG(effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM due
GROUP BY plan, age, start_day
ORDER BY cancels DESC LIMIT 200
''',220)

# Weekly billing due next 91 days total and by age class, current active only
q('billing due next 91 days by week total', f'''
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+91}),
due AS (SELECT d, c.group_id, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, s.effective_price*s.seat_count amt, s.seat_count seats
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(seats) seats, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) due_mrr
FROM due GROUP BY wk ORDER BY wk
''',120)
q('S2 billing due next 91 by week/plan/agebucket', f'''
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+91}),
due AS (SELECT d, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, ((d-s.start_day)/30)*30 age_bucket, s.effective_price amt, s.promotion promo
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, plan, age_bucket, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) due_mrr, ROUND(AVG(amt),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(promo),2) avg_promo
FROM due GROUP BY wk,plan,age_bucket ORDER BY wk,plan,age_bucket
''',500)

# Churn rates by due last 35 days using cancelled vs active for cohorts whose billing anniversary occurred in window.
q('S2 renewal outcomes last 35 by age/plan', f'''
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur-34} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur}),
base AS (
 SELECT d, s.subscription_id, s.status, s.end_day, c.group_id, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, s.effective_price, s.promotion
 FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
 JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND d>=s.start_day+30 AND d-s.start_day IN (30,60,90,120,150,180,210,240,270,300,330,360,390)
   AND (s.status='subscribed' OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day=d))
)
SELECT plan, age, COUNT(*) due_or_cancel, SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancels,
       ROUND(1.0*SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),4) cancel_rate,
       ROUND(AVG(effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM base GROUP BY plan,age ORDER BY plan,age
''',200)

# Cash by ledger weekly since day 350
q('ledger weekly since350', f'''
SELECT ((day-350)/7) wk, MIN(day) start_day, MAX(day) end_day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount
FROM ledger WHERE day>=350 GROUP BY wk,category ORDER BY wk, category
''',500)

# Active open issues only among subscribed customers by group age
q('open issues joined active subscriptions by group age', f'''
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open
''',200)
q('open issue counts active vs nonactive', f'''
SELECT CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 'active_sub' ELSE COALESCE(s.status,'no_sub') END substatus,
       i.group_id, COUNT(*) n, AVG(i.days_open) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY substatus,i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
''',100)

# Current config full latest
q('latest config overrides fullish', '''
SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,settings_json FROM config_overrides
WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type
''',20)

# Enterprise latest non-agent totals count
q('enterprise latest nonagent totals', '''
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id
''',200)

# Latest nonagent details customer ids limited for churn and count for new
q('enterprise churn prevention details', '''
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id, et.day, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.seat_count, c.group_id, s.plan, s.seat_count sub_seats, s.effective_price, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_primary_concern, et.message_text
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE et.sender!='agent' AND et.thread_type!='new_lead'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
''',20)

# Research API
print('\n-- research API --')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(),indent=2)[:12000])
except Exception as e: print(e)

import novamind_api as nm, math, json
cur=nm.vars.current_day
cash=nm.query('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash']
print('cur cash',cur,cash)
# active aggregate
rows=nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, c.customer_type, s.plan, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30,
       COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) eff_mrr,
       SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count) listed_mrr,
       AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff, AVG(s.listed_price) avg_listed, AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30
''')['rows']
print('groups',len(rows))

def churn_rate(g, plan, age):
    # age is exact days at billing
    if g=='S2':
        b=(age//30)*30
        if plan=='A':
            if b<=30: return 1.0
            if b in (60,90,120): return 0.90  # rare/new, assume poor
            if b in (150,180,210,240): return 0.02
            if b==270: return 0.055
            if b==300: return 0.21
            if b==330: return 0.16
            if b>=360: return 0.14
            return 0.10
        if plan=='B':
            if b<=30: return 1.0
            if b in (60,90): return 0.95
            if b==120: return 0.88
            if b==150: return 0.77
            if b==180: return 0.72
            if b==210: return 0.065
            if b==240: return 0.063
            if b==270: return 0.033
            if b==300: return 0.030
            if b==330: return 0.036
            if b>=360: return 0.040
            return 0.05
        if plan=='C':
            if b<=30: return 1.0
            if b in (60,90): return 0.97
            if b==120: return 0.85
            if b==150: return 0.79
            if b==180: return 0.48
            if b>=210: return 0.015
            return 0.05
    if g=='D_S07':
        return 0.02 if plan=='C' else 0.15
    if g=='E3':
        return 0.06
    # other tiny/nonactive groups
    return 0.05

def target_payment_mrr(row):
    g=row['group_id']; plan=row['plan']; seats=row['seats']; listed=row['listed_mrr']; eff=row['eff_mrr']
    if g=='S2':
        promo={'A':10.0,'B':5.0,'C':0.0}.get(plan,0.0)
        return max(0.0, listed - promo*seats)
    if g=='D_S07':
        promo=40.0 + (20.0 if plan=='B' else 0.0)
        return max(0.0, listed - promo*seats)
    # Enterprise negotiated: keep current effective unless accepted offer not modeled
    return eff

# state list mutable with fractional survival
state=[]
for r in rows:
    state.append(dict(r))

# per-active daily compute cost calibrated from last-week active usage
compute_per_sub_day={'A':2.352/7.0,'B':7.08/7.0,'C':35.35/7.0}
fixed_ops_day=5000+52675
fixed_dev_day=3000+52400
capacity_day=10000
lead_cost_week=1100
weekly=[]
for wk in range(1,27):
    start=cur+(wk-1)*7+1; end=cur+wk*7
    pay=0.0; churned_mrr=0.0; churned_n=0.0
    compute=0.0
    for d in range(start,end+1):
        # daily compute on active current state before churn at billing
        for r in state:
            if r['group_id']=='S2':
                compute += (r['n'] or 0)*compute_per_sub_day.get(r['plan'],0.0)
            elif r['group_id']=='D_S07':
                compute += (r['n'] or 0)*compute_per_sub_day.get(r['plan'],0.0)
            elif r['customer_type']=='large':
                compute += 30.0/7.0  # tiny calibrated placeholder per enterprise customer-week
        # process billings at end of day
        for r in state:
            if int(r['billing_day_mod30']) == d%30 and r['n']>1e-9:
                age=d-int(r['start_day'])
                if age>=30:
                    cr=churn_rate(r['group_id'], r['plan'], age)
                else:
                    cr=0.0
                # clamp: endgame veterans slightly safer from tenure; price harvest B +5 adds small risk
                if r['group_id']=='S2' and r['plan']=='B' and age>=300:
                    cr += 0.005  # B promo trim risk
                cr=max(0,min(1,cr))
                survive=1-cr
                bill=target_payment_mrr(r)*survive
                pay += bill
                # reduce state by churn
                churned_mrr += r['eff_mrr']*cr
                churned_n += r['n']*cr
                for key in ('n','seats','eff_mrr','listed_mrr'):
                    r[key] *= survive
    # costs; after final horizon maybe keep same rules but subscribers have dwindled
    costs=compute + 7*(fixed_ops_day+fixed_dev_day+capacity_day) + lead_cost_week
    net=pay-costs
    weekly.append({'wk':wk,'pay':pay,'compute':compute,'costs':costs,'net':net,'churn_n':churned_n,'end_mrr':sum(r['eff_mrr'] for r in state)})

cum=cash
for w in weekly:
    cum += w['net']
    w['cash']=cum
print('weekly forecast')
for w in weekly[:13]:
    print({k: (round(v,2) if isinstance(v,float) else v) for k,v in w.items()})
print('horizons')
for h in [1,4,12,26]:
    print(h, weekly[h-1]['cash'])
